steps.title: Identify the subject's number
steps.content: The subject of the sentence is “an artificial intelligence”—singular. The relative clause “that absorbs sustainable energies” and the participial phrase “redistributing them through intricate networks...” both modify the subject but don't change its number. The subject remains singular.

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement
steps.content: A singular subject requires a singular present-tense verb. “Powers” (C) is the only singular present form. Options A and D use plural verbs, and B uses a base form that fails to agree with the singular subject. C is correct.
